summary: Started stage-system decompilation by replacing the first small stage camera accessor routines from asm/melee/gr/stage.s with matching C in src/melee/gr/stage.c, adding a provisional stage.h with StageInfo, StageCameraInfo, and StageBlastZone layouts, and globally renaming the data symbol lbl_8049E6C8 to stage_info across many asm users. The PR intentionally kept function names generic because the author considered available community documentation for the related stage data inconsistent, and stage.h explicitly warns that the camera struct is likely not fully correct.
what_changed: Added src/melee/gr/stage.c with C implementations of func_80224A54, func_80224A68, func_80224A80, func_80224A98, func_80224AB0, func_80224AC8, and func_80224AD8.
The matched C functions are simple accessors over stage_info.cam_info: camera bounds plus x/y offsets, camera pan degrees multiplied by lbl_804DBCD0, max depth, and zoom rate.
Added src/melee/gr/stage.h defining StageCameraInfo, StageBlastZone, and StageInfo with offset comments for the inferred layout.
Removed the corresponding seven assembly implementations from asm/melee/gr/stage.s while leaving later stage.s functions in asm.
Renamed the global data label lbl_8049E6C8 to stage_info in asm/melee/text_2.s and many stage/fighter/camera/mode asm references.
Updated include/variables.h from a placeholder lbl_8049E6C8 comment to stage_info.
Added src/melee/gr/stage.o to obj_files.mk so the new C translation unit participates in the build.
smart_moves: Matched low-risk leaf accessors first; each function maps directly to a small number of loads/adds/multiplies, making stage decomp progress without taking on complex control flow.
Established a named global, stage_info, before fully decompiling all users; this makes future diffs and cross-system references easier to search than raw lbl_8049E6C8.
Preserved uncertainty in the header rather than overfitting names from inconsistent external documentation.
Used offset comments in the provisional structs, making it easier to validate future field names against assembly accesses such as 0x88 for internal_stage_id and 0x74-0x80 for blast-zone fields.
Kept unknown fields as x20, x24, x3C, x84, and trailing data instead of inventing semantic names unsupported by the diff.
decomp_lessons: For early subsystem matching, start by naming the central data object and matching simple accessor functions; even partial structs immediately improve readability across many asm files.
When external documentation conflicts with observed layout, prefer conservative field names and comments explaining the uncertainty.
Offset-annotated structs are valuable in mixed C/asm phases because remaining asm users can still be checked against the inferred C layout.
Do not force meaningful function names when the behavior or surrounding domain model is not yet clear; address-based func_ names are acceptable until stronger evidence appears.
A single global rename can touch many asm files but is useful if it reflects a real shared data structure used by multiple systems.
naming_conventions: Introduced PascalCase typedefs StageCameraInfo, StageBlastZone, and StageInfo.

Used snake_case field names for understood stage camera and blast-zone fields, such as cam_bounds_left, cam_x_offset, cam_pan_degrees, cam_zoom_rate, cam_max_depth, internal_stage_id, and blast_zone.left/right/top/bottom.
Used xNN-style placeholders such as x20, x24, x3C, and x84 for unknown fields.
Retained address-based function names func_80224A54 through func_80224AD8 because the PR body says the author decided not to name the functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched accessor functions by expressing lfs/fadds patterns as direct struct field reads and additions in C.
Matched func_80224AB0 by preserving the external float constant lbl_804DBCD0 and multiplying it by stage_info.cam_info.cam_pan_degrees.
Replaced assembly references to lbl_8049E6C8@ha/@l with stage_info@ha/@l without changing instruction bytes or control flow.

Used a C struct layout that preserves exact offsets needed by existing assembly references, including camera fields at 0x00-0x70, blast-zone fields at 0x74-0x80, x84, internal_stage_id at 0x88, and trailing data.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
The only explanatory human text is from the PR body: the author disliked inconsistent community documentation for the tied data and therefore avoided naming the functions.
The PR body also notes a camera struct comment explaining why the struct is probably wrong; stage.h contains that caveat.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that refine StageCameraInfo or replace x20/x24/x3C/x84 placeholder fields.
Search for stage_info internal_stage_id uses to determine enum names for stage IDs.
Look for later matches of func_80224AE8 and neighboring stage.s accessors using the same StageInfo layout.
Search for grGroundParam references to compare against the stage.h caveat about mismatched community documentation.
Find later PRs that rename func_80224A54 through func_80224AD8 after their semantics become clearer.
